findings.requirement: US-2: .gitignore includes .env, *.key, secrets/, .idea/ and other local files
findings.detail: Tech-spec Acceptance Criteria list .gitignore entries: backend/build/, backend/.gradle/, frontend/node_modules/, frontend/.nuxt/, .env, *.key, secrets/ — but .idea/ is absent. Task 1 description also omits .idea/ (says 'Java/Gradle/Node/Nuxt artifact patterns and secrets'). The user-spec explicitly names .idea/ as a required entry. Without it being in the AC or task scope, the implementing agent may skip it.

findings.requirement: US-11: .env.example contains MONGODB_URI and REDIS_URL with comments; no other variables
findings.detail: Tech-spec mentions '.env.example (2 vars)' in Architecture and Task 1 says 'Create .env.example with MONGODB_URI and REDIS_URL only'. The user-spec requires both variables to have explanatory comments. The tech-spec never states the comments requirement, so the implementing agent has no explicit instruction to include them.

summary: 13/15 requirements fully covered, 2 partial, 0 missing. Both partials are minor: .idea/ omitted from tech-spec gitignore AC, and .env.example comments requirement not carried over into tech-spec. No scope creep, no overengineering, no underengineering, no undocumented deviations. All 3 user-spec deviations (Spring Boot 3.4.x, Nuxt 4, devtools) are documented with reasons and marked approved. Solution section provides genuine technical substance (WebFlux security bootstrapping rationale, @MockBean strategy). Tech-spec passes all critical checks.
